name: Admission Gate
purpose: Independently classify, inspect, review, contain, and admit repository mutations without granting candidate code authority over its own acceptance.

interfaces.name: trusted-static-admission
interfaces.contract: static-gate.mjs receives a candidate directory, GitHub event JSON, and changed-file inventory; it emits an acceptance decision, findings, and risk classification without executing candidate code.

interfaces.name: adversarial-review-admission
interfaces.contract: codex-gate-cli.mjs fails closed for high or critical mutations until an owner-approved reviewer identity or temporary owner-controlled review label supplies valid evidence.

interfaces.name: automatic-merge-arming
interfaces.contract: Non-critical pull requests may have squash auto-merge armed; GitHub branch rules and required checks remain the final merge lock.

security.network: Privileged workflows use GitHub API access only. Candidate execution is networkless. Dependency resolution remains a controlled bootstrap exception and cannot include community dependency changes.
security.filesystem: Privileged inspection reads candidate files as untrusted data. Candidate runtime receives a read-only repository mount and disposable tmpfs.
security.subprocesses: Trusted Node.js admission tools, GitHub CLI metadata reads, Docker runtime, and candidate test commands inside the restricted container.
security.secrets: No repository secrets are exposed to pull-request code. Privileged workflows use least-privilege GITHUB_TOKEN permissions and never execute candidate code.

extension_points: Bind the exact installed Codex reviewer identity after owner verification.
Move policy and scanner logic into a private observer repository or GitHub App.
Add behavioral syscall and outbound-connection observation.
Replace temporary owner review labels with signed Agent Gateway attestations.
